steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: Text 2's central claim: Bill Monroe created bluegrass by combining Southern folk music with commercial genres (jazz and blues), directly contradicting the purist assumption that bluegrass emerged from pure folk traditions.

steps.title: Match to the answer
steps.content: Since Monroe mixed genres at bluegrass's origin, the purists' insistence on folk-based purity is illogical—they overlook how their genre actually came into existence. The other choices address enforcement ability, personal hypocrisy, or audience strategy—not the logical flaw rooted in historical fact. That matches choice D — D is correct.
